Bibliography & References¶

Copies of the academic papers and reference charts used to build the DrumScript engine are provided available below.

Academic Papers¶

Links are embedded

  • Beat Tracking by Dynamic Programming

  • Maximum Filter Vibrato Suppression for Onset Detection Onset detection algorithms, vibration suppression

  • libf0: A Python Library for Frequency Estimation

  • Hybrid Transformers for Music Source Separation
    The foundational paper for the Demucs engine.

  • Real-Time Automatic Drum Transcription
    Research on dynamic few-shot learning for drums.

  • Improving Perceptual Quality of Drum Transcription
    Google Magenta’s research on the Expanded Groove MIDI Dataset.

  • Signal Processing for Music Analysis Beat, digital signal processing, harmony, melody, music analysis, music information retrieval, music signals, pitch, rhythm, source separation, timbre, voice separation.

  • Transcription and Separation of Drum Signals From Polyphonic Music Drum signals, feature selection, harmonic/noise decomposition, music transcription, source separation, support vector machine (SVM), Wiener filtering.

  • An algorithm for detecting and labeling drum events in polyphonic music Drum detection, onset detection, feature extraction, support vector machines, audio-to-MIDI.

  • MT3: Multi-Task Multi-Track Music Transcription Google MT3 Transcription Engine paper.

  • Increasing Drum Transcription Vocabulary Using Data Synthesis

  • Sequence-to-Sequence Piano Transcription with Transformers Magenta Lab, Google Research.

  • Computational Models of Similarity for Drum Samples Content-based similarity, drum sounds, percussive music instruments.

  • librosa: Audio and Music Signal Analysis in Python audio, music, signal processing, python, DSP

  • Basic Pitch: A Lightweight Instrument-Agnostic Model for Polyphonic Note Transcription and Multi-Pitch Estimation Automatic Music Transcription, AMT, note estimation, multi-pitch estimation, polyphonic, low-resource

  • Keep the beat going: Automatic drum transcription with momentum Automatic Drum Transcription, ADT

Standards & Charts¶

  • General MIDI Percussion Key Map
    The standard mapping of notes to drum sounds (e.g., 36 = Kick).

  • Midi Frequency Table Source: Note names, MIDI numbers and frequencies(https://www.phys.unsw.edu.au/jw/notes.html)

  • Master Drum Key
    A comprehensive guide to standard notation symbols. Source:Master-Drum-Key_R2

  • Note Frequency Chart (Pitch to Note)

  • How to Read Drum Music Notation (Gear4music)
